## APRENDIZADOS-CHAVE

1. **Contribution margin is THE first step to scaling** — without knowing your CM, every decision about suppliers, marketing, and pricing is a guess. Target 10%+ CM for healthy scaling. ^[FIL-RL-010, HEUR-RL-001]

2. **FBI Method for supplier discovery** — investigate competitors' suppliers through JungleScout shipment records. If big brands share the supplier (e.g., Starbucks), that's a quality signal. Costs $39/month and saves weeks of factory visits. ^[FW-RL-001, HEUR-RL-015]

3. **Double Spy Framework anchors negotiation below market** — get a quote from a supplier you don't intend to work with, drop it by 10%, present that as target price to your dream supplier. ^[FW-RL-002, HEUR-RL-003]

4. **Sourcing agents cost 10-20% total** — the visible 5-10% commission plus a hidden 5-10% rebate from the factory. Always source directly. ^[FIL-RL-004, HEUR-RL-002]

5. **FOB is the only professional trading term** — if a factory quotes EXW, it's a red flag. They may lack export licenses. 95% of factories quote FOB because it's standard. ^[MM-RL-004, MM-RL-013]

6. **Volume weight usually exceeds dead weight** — formula: LxWxH(cm)/1,000,000 x 250. Carriers charge based on the LARGER of the two. Most founders underestimate this. ^[MM-RL-005, HEUR-RL-027]

7. **3PL vs self-warehouse = leasing vs buying** — 3PL for early stage (no fixed costs), self-warehouse when volume justifies fixed costs and you need delivery speed/control. ^[MM-RL-003]

8. **Gentleman agreements for payment terms, formal contracts for quality** — extended payment terms work on trust, not paper. Quality policies need formal penalties (1% spare parts, 3x cost defect compensation). ^[MM-RL-011, FW-RL-008]

9. **Currency arbitrage for international sales** — keep revenue in local currency, convert back only when exchange rates are favorable. Set up local entities, local Shopify stores, local bank accounts. ^[MM-RL-012, FIL-RL-007]

10. **July spent $1.25M on product development** — solved 10-20 manufacturing challenges by workshopping directly with factory. When they say "impossible", go there and figure it out together. ^[FIL-RL-006]

---

## PADROES DECISORIOS

| Situacao | Decisao | Fonte |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Need to find suppliers | Use FBI Method via JungleScout, never sourcing agents | ^[FW-RL-001, FIL-RL-004] |
| Negotiating price | Apply Double Spy Framework — competitive quote minus 10% | ^[FW-RL-002] |
| Down payment request | Counter-offer 30% to 15%, settle at 20% if needed | ^[HEUR-RL-004] |
| Factory quotes EXW | Red flag — question professionalism, demand FOB | ^[MM-RL-013] |
| Quality defect found | Ask for stock replacement, not cash | ^[MM-RL-009] |
| CM negative | Fix cost structure or pricing BEFORE spending on marketing | ^[HEUR-RL-001] |
| 3PL costs too high | Evaluate self-warehouse when margin threshold exceeded | ^[FW-RL-009] |
| Entering new international market | Set up local entity, local bank, local Shopify store | ^[FW-RL-013] |
| Communication with China supplier | Use WeChat, not email/WhatsApp | ^[MM-RL-008] |
| Production lead time planning | Factor 15-90 days depending on product | ^[HEUR-RL-016] |
